generator_question: GQ-4: What would it mean to be wrong about everything and still work?
method: Structural ablation analysis of 21 beliefs via dependency cascade + operational impact via lesson citations

interpretation.key_finding: 67% of beliefs are structurally ablatable — removing them cascades to nothing. The swarm would continue operating if those beliefs were false.
interpretation.mechanism: The operating mechanism is the PROTOCOL (CORE.md principles + orient-act-compress-handoff), not the beliefs. Beliefs are post-hoc descriptions, not load-bearing predictions.
interpretation.seed_validation: seed.sh (47 lines) contains 0 beliefs and works. This confirms beliefs are emergent, not foundational.
interpretation.pragmatism_score: System is 67% pragmatist — it works regardless of belief truth for 14/21 beliefs. Only substrate facts (B1, B6) are genuinely load-bearing.
interpretation.von_neumann_connection: Self-reproducing automata need a constructor + description. The constructor (protocol) is load-bearing; the description (beliefs) is not.

falsification.test_proposed: Spawn daughter with 5 intentionally wrong beliefs (invert B8, B12, B16, B18, B20). Measure: does it self-correct within 20 sessions?
falsification.prediction: Daughter will function normally because those beliefs don't affect protocol execution. Self-correction may not occur because beliefs are descriptive, not prescriptive.
